hypothesis: If a convolutional neural network is first pretrained on a large data-rich domain of additive-free SEM images before fine-tuning on a small data-poor domain of additive-containing samples, then the resulting transfer learning model will achieve significantly higher classification accuracy across NCM compositions and aging states compared to models trained without this two-stage pretraining strategy, because the source domain pretraining enables the network to learn generalizable morphological feature representations that compensate for the scarcity of labeled target domain data.
